ExperimentTitle: E16 (Reverse Bias): I–V characteristics of a p-n junction diode
Aim: To draw the reverse-bias I–V characteristic curve of a p-n junction diode.
Procedure: Close the key and increase the reverse voltage slowly (0 to -12V).
Note the reverse current (µA) and reverse voltage across the diode.
Use the supply voltmeter to verify the applied reverse voltage.
Plot the reverse I–V curve (flat leakage region expected).

Components.Name: VV1
Components.Title: VariableVoltage (-12–0V)
Components.Description: DC supply for reverse bias (negative sweep)

Components.Name: K1
Components.Title: Plug Key (K)
Components.Description: Main circuit key (uses negative terminal for reverse bias)

Components.Name: Am1
Components.Title: Microammeter
Components.Description: Measures reverse current in µA range

Components.Name: VmSupply
Components.Title: Voltmeter (Supply)
Components.Description: Measures battery voltage between pos and pos1 (moved left to avoid slider obstruction).

Components.Name: NodeDin
Components.Title: Diode Left Node (Visual Anchor)
Components.Description: Zero-ohm connector chain to create a physical anchor node for the left diode terminal.

Components.Name: NodeDout
Components.Title: Diode Right Node (Visual Anchor)
Components.Description: Zero-ohm connector chain to create a physical anchor node for the right diode terminal.

Components.Name: D1
Components.Title: Diode (1N4007)
Components.Description: Explicit 1N4007 diode model params [bv, ibv, is, n, rs] (REVERSED for reverse-bias experiment)

Components.Name: Connect1
Components.Title: Junction
Components.Description: Main junction connector used for wiring.
